segments.speaker: Gallerist
segments.text: Hi, let me know if you have any questions.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Thanks. I was actually wondering about this piece. Is it part of a larger series?

segments.speaker: Gallerist
segments.text: Yes, the artist has been working with domestic objects and memory.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: That makes sense. It feels familiar, but slightly displaced.

segments.speaker: Gallerist
segments.text: That's a nice way to put it.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I'm studying architecture, so I'm also interested in how the work changes the room.

segments.speaker: Gallerist
segments.text: The installation was designed specifically for this space.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I noticed the sightline from the entrance. It pulls you in before you understand what you're looking at.

segments.speaker: Gallerist
segments.text: Exactly. The artist wanted that slow reveal.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: It's really thoughtful. Is there an artist statement I could read?

segments.speaker: Gallerist
segments.text: Yes, right by the front desk.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Great. Thank you. This is giving me a lot to think about.
